[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Paiute%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Paiute%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Paiute%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Paiute%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(September 2025)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

([/ˈpaɪjuːt/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); also **Piute**) refers to three non-contiguous groups of [Indigenous peoples of the Great Basin](/wiki/Indigenous_peoples_of_the_Great_Basin "Indigenous peoples of the Great Basin"). Although their languages are related within the [Numic](/wiki/Numic_languages "Numic languages") group of [Uto-Aztecan languages](/wiki/Uto-Aztecan_languages "Uto-Aztecan languages"), these three languages do not form a single subgroup and they are no more closely related to each than they are to the Central Numic languages ([Timbisha](/wiki/Timbisha_language "Timbisha language"), [Shoshoni](/wiki/Shoshoni_language "Shoshoni language"), and [Comanche](/wiki/Comanche_language "Comanche language")) which are spoken between them.

showing the classification differences between the various groups called "Paiute" with the *Western Numic* branch of languages containing [Mono](/wiki/Mono_language_(California) "Mono language (California)") with its [Owens Valley Paiute](/wiki/Mono_people#Eastern_Mono_(Owens_Valley_Paiute) "Mono people") dialect and [Northern Paiute](/wiki/Northern_Paiute_language "Northern Paiute language") on the left and the *Southern Numic* branch containing the [Colorado River language](/wiki/Colorado_River_Numic_language "Colorado River Numic language") and its *[Southern Paiute](/wiki/Southern_Paiute_people "Southern Paiute people")* dialect on the right. [Mutually intelligible](/wiki/Mutual_intelligibility "Mutual intelligibility") groups of dialects are grouped together at the bottom of each branch in *italics*.

Canaday, Timothy W. (2001). [*High-altitude Archeological investigations at Cedar Breaks National Monument, Utah*](https://archive.org/details/highaltitudearch00cana/page/18/mode/2up?q=Paiute). U.S. Department of the Interior, National Park Service.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/84/Paiute_map.svg/960px-Paiute_map.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Paiute_map.svg "Historic map of tribes called \"Paiute\" overlaid on a map of present-day western US state borders")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/84/Paiute_map.svg/960px-Paiute_map.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Paiute_map.svg)

Historic map of tribes called "Paiute" overlaid on a map of present-day western US state borders

**Paiute** ([/ˈpaɪjuːt/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"); also **Piute**) refers to three non-contiguous groups of [Indigenous peoples of the Great Basin](/wiki/Indigenous_peoples_of_the_Great_Basin "Indigenous peoples of the Great Basin"). Although their languages are related within the [Numic](/wiki/Numic_languages "Numic languages") group of [Uto-Aztecan languages](/wiki/Uto-Aztecan_languages "Uto-Aztecan languages"), these three languages do not form a single subgroup and they are no more closely related to each than they are to the Central Numic languages ([Timbisha](/wiki/Timbisha_language "Timbisha language"), [Shoshoni](/wiki/Shoshoni_language "Shoshoni language"), and [Comanche](/wiki/Comanche_language "Comanche language")) which are spoken between them.

The term "Paiute" does not refer to a single, unique, unified group of Great Basin tribes, but is a historical label comprising:

* [Northern Paiute people](/wiki/Northern_Paiute_people "Northern Paiute people") of northeastern California, northwestern Nevada, eastern Oregon, and southern Idaho
* [Southern Paiute people](/wiki/Southern_Paiute_people "Southern Paiute people") of northern Arizona, southern Nevada, and southwestern Utah[[1]](#cite_note-1)
* [Mono people](/wiki/Mono_people "Mono people") of east central California, divided into [Owens Valley Paiute](/wiki/Mono_people#Eastern_Mono_(Owens_Valley_Paiute) "Mono people") (Eastern Mono) and [Western Mono](/wiki/Mono_people#Western_Mono "Mono people") (Monache)

## Linguistic differences

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Paiute&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Linguistic differences")]

Though sharing similar Numic roots, the three groups historically called Paiute have different languages as shown below:

|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Numic](/wiki/Numic_languages "Numic languages") | |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | |  | |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | *Western* | |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | [Northern Paiute](/wiki/Northern_Paiute_language "Northern Paiute language") | |  | |  | [Mono](/wiki/Californian_Mono_language "Californian Mono language") | |  | | |  | | *Southern* | |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | [Kawaiisi](/wiki/Kawaiisu_language "Kawaiisu language") | |  | |  | [Southern Paiute](/wiki/Colorado_River_Numic_language "Colorado River Numic language") | |  | | |  | | |  | |
|  |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/90/Numic_tree.svg/960px-Numic_tree.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Numic_tree.svg)

Tree of **[Numic languages](/wiki/Numic_languages "Numic languages")** showing the classification differences between the various groups called "Paiute" with the *Western Numic* branch of languages containing [Mono](/wiki/Mono_language_(California) "Mono language (California)") with its [Owens Valley Paiute](/wiki/Mono_people#Eastern_Mono_(Owens_Valley_Paiute) "Mono people") dialect and [Northern Paiute](/wiki/Northern_Paiute_language "Northern Paiute language") on the left and the *Southern Numic* branch containing the [Colorado River language](/wiki/Colorado_River_Numic_language "Colorado River Numic language") and its *[Southern Paiute](/wiki/Southern_Paiute_people "Southern Paiute people")* dialect on the right. [Mutually intelligible](/wiki/Mutual_intelligibility "Mutual intelligibility") groups of dialects are grouped together at the bottom of each branch in *italics*.
